import {
  Box,
  Text,
  ListTextIcon,
  Button,
  InputBox,
  Icon,
  BackDropBottom,
} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
import React, { FC } from 'react';
import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
import {
  formatCurrencyBR,
  getFormattedPoints,
  getParsedCurrency,
  getParsePoints,
} from '../../utils/functions';
import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';

const InvoiceCashbackPointsView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
  handleGoBack,
  handleContinue,
  points,
  formattedPrice,
  handleChangeReais,
  handleChangePoints,
  handleUseAll,
  pointsValue,
  reaisValue,
  convertionValue,
  pointsError,
  reaisError,
  items,
  pointsQuantity,
  handleModalizeContinue,
  onOpenDefault,
  modalizeRef1,
  handleSelectEvent,
  height,
  width,
  isLoadingParameterization,
}) => {
  return (
    <BasicScreen
      hasScroll
      hasHeader
      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
      title="Cashback na fatura"
      hasRightIcon
      rightIcon="icon-help"
      rightIconOnPress={onOpenDefault}
      errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
      isLoading={false}
    >
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
        <Text
          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
          type="headingMDL"
          mt="smaller"
        >
          Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura
        </Text>

        <Box direction="row" mb="smaller" mt="nano" align="center">
          <Text type="paragraphMD" mr="quark">
            Pontos disponíveis:
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphXXXL" mr="quark">
            {getFormattedPoints(points || 0)}
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphMD">({formattedPrice})</Text>
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em pontos"
          placeholder="0"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="number-pad"
          value={pointsValue}
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_pontos')}
          onChangeText={handleChangePoints}
          error={pointsError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
          textRight="Usar todos"
          onPressTextRight={handleUseAll}
        />

        <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
          <Icon
            name="icon-switch-vertical"
            size="xxs"
            color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
            mr="nano"
            ml="nano"
          />
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em reais"
          placeholder="R$0,00"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="decimal-pad"
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_valor')}
          value={reaisValue}
          onChangeText={handleChangeReais}
          error={reaisError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
        />
        {isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
          </Box>
        )}
        {!isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Text type="paragraphMD">
              Cada {pointsQuantity || '0'} pontos valem {convertionValue || formatCurrencyBR(0)} de
              cashback na fatura.
            </Text>
          </Box>
        )}
      </Box>
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
        <Button
          disabled={
            pointsValue === '' ||
            reaisValue === '' ||
            pointsValue === '0' ||
            reaisValue === '0' ||
            pointsError ||
            reaisError
          }
          text="Continuar"
          mt="large"
          mb="small"
          onPress={() => {
            const ptsSel = getParsePoints(pointsValue);
            const reaisSel = getParsedCurrency(reaisValue);
            handleContinue({ pointsSelected: ptsSel, reaisSelected: reaisSel });
          }}
          testID="invoice-cashback-button"
        />
      </Box>
      <BackDropBottom modalHeight={height * 0.53} modalizeRef={modalizeRef1}>
        <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller" mt="smaller" align="center">
          Use seus pontos para pagar menos na fatura
        </Text>

        <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="nano" type="list" mb="smaller" />

        <Button text="Entendi" onPress={handleModalizeContinue} />
      </BackDropBottom>
    </BasicScreen>
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackPointsView;

    32→export function setRewardsProgramSlices<T extends RewardsProgramRootStore>(...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>) {

content.text: Now let me check the old Redux implementation to see how handleContinue was originally called:
Good, let me see the rest of the original implementation to understand what was passed:

Now I have enough information. Let me compile the comprehensive review:

## REDUX TO ZUSTAND MIGRATION REVIEW: InvoiceCashbackPoints

**OVERALL RESULT: FAIL** - Critical issues found

---

## Critical Issues

### 1. HANDLECONTINUE FUNCTION SIGNATURE MISMATCH (BREAKING)
**Location**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx` (line 72-90)

**Issue**: The view.tsx calls `handleContinue()` with a **parameter object**, but the index.tsx function expects **no parameters**.

**Current view.tsx (line 144)**:
```tsx
handleContinue({ pointsSelected: ptsSel, reaisSelected: reaisSel })
```

**Current index.tsx (lines 72-90)**:
```tsx
const handleContinue = () => {
  // Uses pointsValue and reaisValue from local state directly
  navigation.navigate('InvoiceCashbackSummary', {
    pointsSelected: getParsePoints(pointsValue),
    reaisSelected: reaisValue,
    conversionFactor,
    ...
  });
};
```

**Root Cause**: The view component was updated to pass parameters to handleContinue, but the index logic was not updated to accept them. This is a **logic change** (unauthorized per migration rules).

**Original Redux behavior**: The function relied on closure over local state variables (`pointsValue`, `reaisValue`), and handleContinue was called without parameters.

**Impact**: 
- Runtime error when button is pressed (unused parameters passed)
- Inconsistent with original Redux implementation behavior

---

### 2. MISSING ZUSTAND SLICE IN ROOT STORE
**Location**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/store/slices.ts`

**Issue**: The `RewardsProgramRootStore` type does NOT include a `pointsBalance` export from the extract slice.

**Current slices.ts (lines 20-27)**:
```typescript
export type RewardsProgramRootStore =
  ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
  PermissionSlice &
  AirlineMilesSlice &
  PointsExtractSlice &    // ← Contains pointsBalance as part of this slice
  PointsProductsSlice &
  RescueSlice &
  MarketplaceSlice;
```

**usePoints.ts hook (lines 17-18)**:
```typescript
const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));
```

**Issue**: While `PointsExtractSlice` *does* export `pointsBalance` (verified in extract.store.ts lines 30-31), this is correct structural composition. The slice composition is valid.

**Verdict on this issue**: Actually VALID - PointsExtractSlice includes pointsBalance correctly.

---

### 3. MISSING requestParameterization DEPENDENCY
**Location**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx` (line 30-32)

**Issue**: `useEffect` calls `requestParameterization()` without it in the dependency array.

**Code**:
```typescript
useEffect(() => {
  requestParameterization();
}, []); // ← Missing dependency
```

**Problem**: This violates React hooks best practices and can cause stale closure issues. While the effect itself calls the callback, the linter would flag this. The useCallback in usePoints.ts provides a stable reference, but it should be declared as a dependency for clarity and correctness.

**Severity**: Medium - Works by accident, but fragile.

---

### 4. INCORRECT VALUE MAPPING FROM usePoints HOOK
**Location**: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx` (lines 21-26)

**Issue**: The destructuring from `usePoints()` does NOT match the required values.

**Current code**:
```typescript
const {
  points,
  pointsQuantity,
  isLoadingParameterization,
  requestParameterization,
} = usePoints();
```

**usePoints hook return signature (lines 47-68)**:
```typescript
return {
  points: pointsBalance.points,                    // ✓ Correct
  isLoadingBalance: ...,
  isErrorBalance: ...,
  
  products: pointsProducts.products,               // ✗ Not used but exported
  isLoadingProducts: ...,
  isErrorProducts: ...,
  
  pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,  // ✓ Correct
  conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,  // ✗ Available but not extracted
  isLoadingParameterization: ...,                  // ✓ Correct
  isErrorParameterization: ...,
  
  requestPointsBalance,                           // ✗ Also available
  requestPointsProducts,                          // ✗ Also available
  requestParameterization,                        // ✓ Correct
  requestInitialData,                             // ✗ Also available
  clear,                                          // ✗ Also available
};
```

**Analysis**: The three values extracted (`points`, `pointsQuantity`, `isLoadingParameterization`, `requestParameterization`) ARE correctly provided by usePoints(). This particular mapping is **VALID**.

However, note that `conversionFactor` is available from the hook but the code gets it from `route.params.conversionFactor` instead (line 54). This is intentional per the route props structure, so it's not an error.

---

## Correct Mappings (VERIFIED)

| Redux → Zustand | Source | Status |
|-----------------|--------|--------|
| `pointsReducer.points` → `points` | `pointsBalance.points` from extract.store | ✓ CORRECT |
| `pointsReducer.pointsQuantity` → `pointsQuantity` | `parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity` | ✓ CORRECT |
| `pointsReducer.isLoadingParameterization` → `isLoadingParameterization` | `parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING` | ✓ CORRECT |
| `requestParameterization` action → `requestParameterization` function | `getParameterPointsProgram()` wrapper | ✓ CORRECT |

---

## Other Findings

### view.tsx Issue
**Location**: Line 144 in view.tsx

The view was refactored to pass parameters to handleContinue:
```tsx
handleContinue({ pointsSelected: ptsSel, reaisSelected: reaisSel })
```

But this conflicts with how it's actually used in index.tsx. The original Redux code in index.tsx called handleContinue with **no parameters** and derived values from local state.

**This represents a LOGIC CHANGE** and violates the migration constraint: "NO LOGIC CHANGES during migration - structure only."

---

## Props Type Migration (✓ CORRECT)

**models/index.ts transition**:

**Before** (Redux):
```typescript
import { ScreenProps } from '../../../routes/Models';
import { mapDispatchToProps, mapStateToProps } from '..';

export interface InvoiceCashbackProps
  extends ScreenProps<'InvoiceCashbackPoints', typeof mapStateToProps, typeof mapDispatchToProps> {}
```

**After** (Zustand):
```typescript
import { NativeStackScreenProps } from '@react-navigation/native-stack';
import { PropsFintechCardRoutes } from '../../../routes/Models';

export interface InvoiceCashbackProps
  extends NativeStackScreenProps<PropsFintechCardRoutes, 'InvoiceCashbackPoints'> {}
```

**Verdict**: ✓ **CORRECT** - Properly removes Redux connect wrapper types and uses native navigation types directly.

---

## Redux References Removal (✓ VERIFIED)

**Removed imports**:
- ✓ `connect` from 'react-redux'
- ✓ `bindActionCreators` from 'redux'
- ✓ `Dispatch` from 'redux'
- ✓ `PointsActions` from redux/ducks
- ✓ `AppReducers` from redux/reducers
- ✓ `mapStateToProps` and `mapDispatchToProps` functions
- ✓ `connect()` wrapper export

**Verdict**: ✓ **COMPLETE** - All Redux infrastructure removed from screen component.

---

## Summary

| Criterion | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **Preserves exact behavior?** | FAIL | handleContinue signature changed |
| **Redux references removed?** | PASS | All Redux imports/exports removed |
| **usePoints hook properly used?** | PASS | Correct destructuring of available values |
| **Value mappings correct?** | PASS | Redux fields correctly mapped to Zustand |
| **Props type updated correctly?** | PASS | Proper transition to NativeStackScreenProps |

---

## FINAL VERDICT: **FAIL - DO NOT MERGE**

### Required Fixes Before Approval:

1. **FIX handleContinue signature** - Either:
   - Option A: Update view.tsx to call `handleContinue()` with no parameters (maintains original behavior)
   - Option B: Update index.tsx handleContinue to accept and use the parameter object (introduces logic change, not permitted)
   - **Recommendation**: Use Option A to preserve original Redux behavior

2. **Add requestParameterization to useEffect dependency array** - Line 32

The migration is structurally sound (types, imports, store access), but the handleContinue logic divergence is a critical behavioral change that violates migration constraints.
